questions.question:
$\text{Ag}_{(s)} | \text{Ag}^+_{(aq)}(0.01\text{M}) || \text{Ag}^+_{(aq)}(0.1\text{M}) | \text{Ag}_{(s)}$
$E^0_{\text{Ag}_{(s)} / \text{Ag}_{(aq)}} = 0.80\text{ Volt}$

options.A: Cell can not function as anode and cathode are of same metal

questions.question:
$E_{\text{cell}} = 0.78\text{ Volt}$ for the following cell.
$\text{Fe}_{(s)} | \text{Fe}^{2+}_{(aq)}(x\text{M}) || \text{Cu}^{2+}_{(aq)}(0.01\text{M}) | \text{Cu}_{(s)}$
$E^0_{\text{Fe/Fe}^{2+}_{(aq)}} = 0.44\text{V}, E^0_{\text{Cu/Cu}^{2+}_{(aq)}} = -0.34\text{V}$
How many Faradays are needed for reduction of $2.5\text{ mole}$ of $\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-}$ into $\text{Cr}^{3+}$?

What is the correct Nernst equation for reaction taking place in the following cell
$\text{Mg}_{(s)} | \text{Mg}^{2+}_{(aq)} || \text{Cl}^-_{(aq)} | \text{Cl}_{2(g)}(1\text{atm}) | \text{Pt}$?
What will be the proportion of moles of metal (Cu : Ni : Ag) at Cathode according to the Second law of Faraday ?

Which Nernst equation is true to find out the potential of non-standard electro-chemical cell from the following ? $\text{Fe}_{(s)} / \text{Fe}^{2+}_{(aq. X M)} // \text{I}^-_{(aq)} / \text{I}_{2(s)} / \text{Pt}$
If $1 \text{ A}$ of current is passed through $\text{CuSO}_4$ solution for $10 \text{ seconds}$, the number of Copper atoms deposited at the cathode will be ..........

The potential of the following cell is $0.34 \text{ volt}$ at $25^\circ\text{C}$. Calculate the standard reduction potential of the Copper half cell. $\text{Pt} / \text{H}_2 (1 \text{ atm}) / \text{H}^+ (1 \text{ M}) // \text{Cu}^{2+} (1 \text{ M}) / \text{Cu}$
Which substance is obtained in the solution on electrolysis of aq. $\text{CuSO}_4$ solution using graphite electrodes ?
Use of Electrolysis is not done in ........

Two different electrolytic cells filled with molten $\text{Cu(NO}_3)_2$ and molten $\text{Al(NO}_3)_3$ respectively are connected in series. When electricity is passed $2.7\text{ gram Al}$ is deposited on electrode. Calculate the weight of Cu deposited on cathode. [Cu = 63.5 ; Al = 27.0 gram . mol$^{-1}$ ]

Which of the following reactions is correct for a given electro chemical cell at $25^{\circ}\text{C}$ ? $\text{Pt} / \text{Br}_{2(g)} / \text{Br}^-_{(aq)} // \text{Cl}^-_{(aq)} / \text{Cl}_{2(g)} / \text{Pt}$.
What will be pH of aqueous solution of electrolyte in electrolytic cell during electrolysis of $\text{CuSO}_4\text{ (aq)}$ between graphite electrodes ?
Which of the following will give $\text{H}_{2(g)}$ at cathode and $\text{O}_{2(g)}$ at anode on electrolysis using platinum electrodes?
Which of the following statement is incorrect with respect to metallic or electronic conductivity?

options.A: metallic conductivity depends on the structure of metal and its characteristics
options.B: metallic conductivity depends on the number of electrons in the valence shell of atom of metal
options.C: The electrical conductivity of metal increases with increase in temperature
options.D: There is no change in the structure of metal during electrical conduction

questions.question: Which of the following is concentration cell?
The resulting solution obtained at the end of electrolysis of concentrated aqueous solution of $\text{NaCl}$ ________.

options.D: the colour of red or blue litmus does not change

questions.question: The value of $E^0_{red}$ for metal A, B and C are $0.34\text{ Volt}, -0.80\text{ Volt}$ and $-0.46\text{ Volt}$ respectively. State the correct order for their ability to act as reducing agent.

Two electrolytic cells containing molten solutions of Nickel chloride & Aluminium chloride are connected in series. If same amount of electric current is passed through them, what will be the weight of Nickel obtained when $18\text{ gm}$ of Aluminium is obtained? (Al - $27\text{ gm/mole}$, Ni - $58.5\text{ gm/mole}$)
The graph of $\sqrt{C} \rightarrow \Lambda_m$ for an aqueous solution of which substance is not obtained as a straight line?
Which option is incorrect for the working cell? $\text{Pt|Cl}_{2(g)}(C_1)\text{|Cl}^-(C_1)\text{||Cl}^-(C_2)\text{|Cl}_{2(g)}(C_2)\text{|Pt}$
The pH of the solution ___________ during the electrolysis of dilute aqueous solution of $\text{CuSO}_4$.
In which metal container, the aqueous solution of $\text{CuSO}_4$ can be stored?

For how much time, $10\text{ ampere}$ electric current should be passed through a dilute aqueous $\text{NiSO}_4$ solution during electrolysis using inert electrode, in order to get $5.85\text{ gm}$ Nickel? [At. mass of $\text{Ni} = 58.5\text{gm}$]

What will be the oxidation potential for the following hydrogen half cell at $1\text{ bar}$ pressure and $25^\circ\text{C}$ temperature? $\text{Pt} | \text{H}_{2(g)} | \text{HCl}_{(aq)} \text{pH} = 3$
$\text{Zn(s)} | \text{Zn}^{2+}_{(aq)}(1\text{M}) || \text{Ni}^{2+}_{(aq)}(1\text{M}) | \text{Ni(s)}$. Which is incorrect for the above given cell?
If one mole electrons is passed through the solutions of $\text{AlCl}_3$, $\text{AgNO}_3$ and $\text{MgSO}_4$, in what ratio Al, Ag and Mg will be deposited at the electrodes?

On the basis of the given following electrode potential, which one is the strongest reducing agent? $E^\circ_{\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-}|\text{Cr}^{3+}} = 1.33\text{ V}$, $E^\circ_{\text{MnO}_4^-|\text{Mn}^{2+}} = 1.51\text{ V}$, $E^\circ_{\text{Br}_2|\text{Br}^-} = 1.09\text{ V}$, $E^\circ_{\text{Zn}^{2+}|\text{Zn}} = -0.76\text{ V}$
For which of the following electrolytes the graph of $\lambda_m$ against $\sqrt{C}$ gives negative slope.

One electrolysis of aqueous solution of a halide of a metal ‘M’ by passing $1.5\text{ ampere}$ current for $10\text{ minutes}$ deposits $0.2938\text{ g}$ of metal. If the atomic mass of the metal is $63\text{ gm/mole}$, then what will be the formula of the metal halide?
Which products are obtained during electrolysis of aqueous solution of sodium chloride?

Using the data given below find out the strongest reducing agent? $E^0_{\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-}/\text{Cr}^{3+}} = 1.33\text{ V}$, $E^0_{\text{Cl}_2/\text{Cl}^-} = 1.36\text{ V}$, $E^0_{\text{MnO}_4^-/\text{Mn}^{2+}} = 1.51\text{ V}$, $E^0_{\text{Cr}^{3+}/\text{Cr}} = -0.74\text{ V}$
Which is symbolic representation for following cell reaction, $\text{Mg}_{(s)} + \text{Cl}_{2(g)} \rightarrow \text{Mg}^{2+}_{(aq)} + 2\text{Cl}^-_{(aq)}$.
How much electricity in terms of Faraday is required for reduction of $2\text{ mol}$ $\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-}$ into $\text{Cr}^{3+}$ in acidic medium?
Which is proper value of $x$ for the following to increase cell potential of $\text{Zn}_{(s)} | \text{Zn}^{2+}_{(xM)} || \text{Cu}^{2+}_{(0.02M)} | \text{Cu}_{(s)}$
Which substance is used as oxidising agent in nickel-cadmium cell?
Resistance of a conductivity cell filled with 0.1 M KCl solution is $100 \, \Omega$ and conductivity of solution is $1.29 \text{ s/m}$. Then what will be the value of conductivity cell constant.

Which of the following is correct Nearst equation for the given electrochemical cell? $\text{Mg(s)}|\text{Mg}^{2+}_{\text{(aq)}} (0.1\text{M}) || \text{Cl}^-_{\text{(aq)}} (0.1\text{M}) | \text{Cl}_{2\text{(g)}} (1\text{bar}) | \text{Pt(s)}$
Which of the following chemical reaction occur at anode during electrolysis of higher concentrated $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ solution?
Calculate the potential of hydrogen electrode in contact with a solution whose pH is 10.
Which of the statements for solution of electrolyte is not correct?

options.A: Conductivity of solution depends on the concentration of electrolyte
options.B: Conductivity of solution depends on the nature of electrolyte
options.C: Conductivity of solution does not depend upon temperature
options.D: Conductivity of solution depends on the nature of solvent and its viscosity

questions.question: During the electrolysis of higher concentration of $	ext{H}_2	ext{SO}_4$, the product obtained at anode is ______.
How much Faraday of electricity is required to reduce 1.5 mole $	ext{KMnO}_4$ into $	ext{MnO}_2$ in basic medium?
Which relation is correct for $\Lambda^0_{m(\text{H}_2\text{O})}$?
For Daniell cell $E^0_{\text{cell}} = 1.1 \text{ V}$. How $K_c$ is represented for reaction occuring in Daniell cell?
For the given reaction how much quantity of electricity in Coulomb is required? $\frac{2}{3} \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 \rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \text{Al} + \text{O}_2$
Which statement is correct for $\Delta G$ and $E_{\text{cell}}$? (For cell reaction)

options.A: $\Delta G$ is intensive and $E_{\text{cell}}$ is extensive property.
options.B: Both $\Delta G$ and $E_{\text{cell}}$ are intensive properties.
options.C: $\Delta G$ is extensive and $E_{\text{cell}}$ is intensive property.
options.D: Both $\Delta G$ and $E_{\text{cell}}$ are extensive properties.
